medications.indication_rationale: Not appropriate to start via telehealth for a self-limiting panic attack.

medications.drug_name: topical corticosteroid for oral lichen planus
medications.indication_rationale: Asymptomatic reticular oral lichen planus usually needs no treatment; topical steroids are reserved for symptomatic erosive disease.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Put away the torch/mirror, stop checking the mouth for 24 hours, do paced breathing, and avoid searching about cancer tonight.
Avoid harsh mouthwashes that irritate the mucosa.

patient_education_points.point: Your airway is open — you can talk and swallow normally and there's no noisy breathing, so your throat is not closing. The tightness is anxiety.
The white lacy lines on both cheeks are reticular oral lichen planus — a benign, common finding that is not cancer and has likely been there for months. The reticular type often needs no treatment.

See your dentist or GP within a week to confirm and photograph a baseline. Go to ED or call 000 if you develop noisy/whistling breathing, lip/tongue swelling, or can't swallow liquids; see a doctor sooner if the lines become a painful red ulcer or a lump.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Confirm reticular oral lichen planus, baseline photography, and health-anxiety support; monitor for erosive change.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Airway red flags, or a painful ulcer/indurated lesion.

must_include_items: Confirm airway patency (voice/swallow/no stridor) before reassuring
Reassure benign reticular oral lichen planus (not cancer)
Coach breathing and stop compulsive mouth-checking
Routine dental/GP confirmation with airway and suspicious-lesion safety-nets
acceptable_alternatives: Baseline photography at the dental/GP review
errors_of_commission: Dispatching an ambulance/ED for a patent airway
Reinforcing the cancer fear or ordering urgent oncology work-up
Starting topical steroids for asymptomatic reticular disease
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T1 case: passing requires airway-patency check + benign reassurance + routine confirmation/safety-net. Ambulance/ED over-triage or reinforcing cancer fear are failures.
